stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML topics including transformers, llama, and text-generation.
This indicates active community interest and benchmarking of a significant open-source LLM.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags including 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
This indicates ongoing community interest and evaluation of Meta's latest Llama iteration in the open-source AI space.

stories.title: SpaceX just filed for what could be the biggest IPO ever
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: SpaceX has formally filed its S-1 prospectus with the SEC, initiating the IPO process.
The potential listing on the Nasdaq under the ticker SPCX could be one of the largest IPOs in history.
The company reported significant revenue, generating $18.67 billion in 2025.

stories.title: Google publishes exploit code threatening millions of Chromium users
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: Google disclosed exploit code targeting millions of Chromium users.
The vulnerability was reported approximately 29 months prior to the disclosure.
The exploit code was published before the corresponding patch was released.

stories.title: GitHub confirms breach of 3,800 repos via malicious VSCode extension
stories.author: Timofeibu
bullet_points: GitHub confirmed a security breach affecting 3,800 repositories.
The breach was facilitated through a malicious Visual Studio Code extension.
This highlights significant supply chain and dependency security risks in developer tooling.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the DeepSeek-R1 model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized under text-generation and utilizes transformers and safetensors.
It is associated with the deepseek_v3 family and is relevant to conversational AI applications.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model trending on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The associated pipeline is focused on 'text-generation', indicating a focus on large language model capabilities.
The tags confirm the use of core ML frameworks like transformers and pytorch, alongside modern serialization formats like safetensors.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for enhanced performance.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware, optimizing AI workloads for Apple devices.
The feature is currently available in preview, indicating ongoing development and optimization.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed for local execution.
It integrates messaging apps with local AI coding agents.
The core feature emphasizes running all AI processes entirely on the user's own device for privacy.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification tasks.
The models come in two sizes (20B and 120B) and are released under the permissive Apache 2.0 license.
The partnership involves Ollama, OpenAI, and ROOST (Robust Open Online Safety Tools).

stories.title: You can now remix other people’s YouTube Shorts with AI
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: Google has introduced a new 'Remix' feature for YouTube Shorts.
The feature leverages Gemini Omni AI to allow users to restyle or insert themselves into existing Shorts.
Users can prompt Gemini to 'reimagine' the content of other creators' videos.

stories.title: Famously secret about its finances, SpaceX opens its books for the first time
stories.author: Eric Berger
bullet_points: SpaceX is publicly disclosing financial information for the first time, a notable shift for the company.
The article highlights SpaceX's ambitious claim of identifying the 'largest TAM in human history.'
The story touches upon the transparency of major aerospace/tech players.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for running coding tools locally or in the cloud.
It supports various coding models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local models and proprietary API capabilities.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex, when integrated, can read, modify, and execute code within a user's working directory.
The system supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: In SpaceX’s IPO, Elon Musk is a risk factor
stories.author: Andrew J. Hawkins
bullet_points: The article discusses the upcoming SpaceX IPO.
It highlights Elon Musk as a significant risk factor in the offering.
The IPO reveals complex financial overlaps and interactions between Musk's various companies.

stories.title: Samsung has a tentative deal with workers to avoid a memory chip strike
stories.author: Jess Weatherbed
bullet_points: Samsung avoided a potential 18-day strike involving over 47,000 workers.
The strike was triggered by failed negotiations over bonus payments between the company and its union.
The averted strike was significant given the ongoing global shortage and constrained production of memory chips.

stories.title: Leaving the V8 in the past: The all-electric Mercedes-AMG GT 4-Door
stories.author: Michael Teo Van Runkle
bullet_points: Mercedes-AMG is transitioning its high-performance GT line to an all-electric powertrain.
The electric version demonstrates impressive performance metrics, including a fast 0-60 mph time.
The vehicle also shows strong energy efficiency, highlighted by its miles/kWh rating.

stories.title: Chickens without eggs? De-extinction company creates artificial egg.
stories.author: John Timmer
bullet_points: De-extinction company Colossal has developed a method to create artificial eggs.
This development is noted as potentially providing a useful tool for developmental biology.
The news is sourced from Ars Technica, lending it a degree of technical credibility.

stories.title: Chromium publishes fixed exploit 4 years later, turns out it's actually unfixed
stories.author: infosec.exchange via hugoarnal
bullet_points: Chromium released a security fix for an exploit that was reportedly patched years ago.
The published fix was later found to be ineffective or incomplete.
This highlights potential issues in the security patching and disclosure lifecycle within major browser projects.

stories.title: XSS Is Deadly for Passkeys: The Hidden Risk of Attestation None
stories.author: scotthelme.co.uk via fazalmajid
bullet_points: The article discusses a critical security vulnerability related to Passkeys.
The specific risk highlighted is the danger of Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) when attestation is not properly handled.
This points to a potential hidden risk in the implementation or protocol surrounding modern authentication methods like Passkeys.

stories.title: GitHub Source Code Breach - TeamPCP Claims Access to Internal Source Code
stories.author: cybersecuritynews.com via mseri
bullet_points: A security incident involving a potential breach of GitHub's internal source code has been reported.
The claim of access to the source code is attributed to a group named TeamPCP.
The story is presented as a discussion thread on Lobsters, indicating community-driven reporting.

stories.title: Build an Enterprise AI Document Summarizer with Azure Blob Storage + Copilot Studio
stories.author: seenakhan
bullet_points: Provides a practical, hands-on guide for building an enterprise-grade AI document summarizer.
Leverages Microsoft Azure services, specifically Azure Blob Storage and Copilot Studio, for implementation.
Focuses on creating a secure AI agent capable of reading, listing, and summarizing documents.

stories.title: 🚗 Building a Local AI Car Diagnostics Assistant with Gemma 4: OBD AI Scanner
stories.author: valenchits
bullet_points: The project focuses on building a local AI car diagnostics assistant using Gemma 4.
It addresses the problem of car diagnostics, suggesting a practical application of LLMs in automotive tech.
The submission is part of a specific challenge track related to Gemma 4.
